content_profile.learning_objective: Explain how scaled dot-product attention turns token relationships into weighted value aggregation.
content_profile.visual_thesis: Attention is shown as a query-dependent redistribution of value information through normalized token-token affinities.
scope: Introduce Q, K, and V as roles derived from tokens.
Show pairwise query-key comparison as a score field.
Show scaling and softmax as staged transformations from raw scores to weights.
Show values being scaled and merged into a context vector.

entities.label: Input tokens
entities.technical_role: Discrete sequence elements whose representations are contextualized.
entities.meaning: Each token can query other tokens for context.
entities.visualization_need: Provides the viewer's anchor before formula symbols appear.
entities.claim_boundary: Token text is illustrative; the mechanism is about representations.

entities.label: Q
entities.technical_role: Asking role used to compare against keys.
entities.meaning: Query vectors express what each token is looking for.
entities.visualization_need: Must be visually distinct from key and value roles.
entities.claim_boundary: Vectors are shown schematically, not with real model values.

entities.label: K
entities.technical_role: Matching role used in query-key comparison.
entities.meaning: Key vectors express what each token can match.
entities.visualization_need: Must align spatially with query vectors during comparison.

entities.label: V
entities.technical_role: Information carriers that are mixed by attention weights.
entities.meaning: Value vectors carry information into the output context.
entities.visualization_need: Need a flow or stream metaphor during aggregation.
entities.claim_boundary: Stream thickness encodes toy weights, not source model activations.

entities.label: QK^T scores
entities.meaning: Each cell is a query-key comparison score.
entities.claim_boundary: Numeric values are illustrative unless explicitly computed from toy vectors.

entities.label: Attention weights
entities.technical_role: Non-negative normalized weights that sum to one.
entities.claim_boundary: Only the sum-to-one relationship is exact in the educational toy example.

entities.label: Context vector
entities.meaning: The output token representation includes weighted context.
entities.visualization_need: A merge target makes the final payoff visible.
entities.claim_boundary: Shown as one vector for one query position.

operators.label: Role projection
operators.technical_meaning: Each token representation is projected into Q, K, and V roles.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Split each token into three same-origin role cards.

operators.label: Query-key comparison
operators.technical_meaning: Queries compare against keys to produce scores.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Trace comparison lines into heatmap cells.

operators.label: Scale scores
operators.technical_meaning: Divide scores by sqrt(d_k) to prevent over-sharp logits.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Cool heatmap intensity while the scale term appears.

operators.label: Softmax normalization
operators.technical_meaning: Convert scaled scores into non-negative proportions.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Transform raw bars into normalized bars with a sum-to-one marker.

operators.label: Weighted value sum
operators.technical_meaning: Scale values by attention weights and merge them.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Route value streams with thickness proportional to weights into the context vector.

states.label: Tokens ready
states.description: A sentence strip with one active token asking for context.

states.label: Q/K/V roles ready
states.description: Each token has stable query, key, and value role cards.

states.label: Raw pairwise scores
states.description: A heatmap contains illustrative query-key relevance scores.

states.label: Scaled scores
states.description: The score field is visually cooled after scaling.

states.label: Attention weights
states.description: Bars represent a normalized distribution over source tokens.

states.label: Context output
states.description: Weighted value streams have merged into a context vector.

relations.meaning: Queries are role projections from token representations.
Query-key comparisons populate the score matrix.
Softmax normalizes scores into a distribution.
Values are aggregated according to attention weights.

invariants.statement: Q, K, V, scores, softmax, and context use stable colors across all beats.
invariants.visual_test: No scene swaps the role colors.

invariants.statement: Attention weights should be shown as proportions that sum to one after softmax.
invariants.visual_test: The normalized bar scene includes a sum-to-one cue.

invariants.statement: Attention is not a hard selection of one token.
invariants.visual_test: Multiple nonzero arcs, bars, and value streams remain visible.

assumptions.statement: All displayed numeric scores and weights are illustrative toy values.
assumptions.impact_on_visualization: Numerical labels may be shown only as pedagogical examples, not source model outputs.

assumptions.statement: The explanation focuses on one query position at a time.
assumptions.impact_on_visualization: The full matrix is introduced through one active row before generalizing.

spaces.purpose: Introduce tokens and active query source.

spaces.coordinate_system: rows for Q, K, and V roles

spaces.purpose: Show weighted values merging into output context.
spaces.depth_semantics: Depth is only used to separate overlapping streams, not as a model dimension.

objects.visual_form: rectangular token tiles with one active query tile
objects.renderer_notes: Manim can render labels; AI video base layer must remain text-free.

objects.visual_form: role cards colored by Q, K, and V
objects.renderer_notes: Use deterministic text rendering for Q, K, and V labels.

objects.visual_form: query-key heatmap with stable row/column semantics
objects.renderer_notes: Heatmap values should be deterministic toy values.

objects.visual_form: normalized bar chart with sum-to-one label
objects.renderer_notes: Use toy weights that sum to one exactly.

objects.visual_form: colored value streams with thickness tied to attention weights
objects.renderer_notes: Manim stream thickness should follow weight ordering; cinematic base can be text-free.
Never delegate formula rendering to AI video.

encodings.risk_note: Color drift would break symbol consistency.
Should be presented as toy values, not measured activations.
Thicker stream means larger contribution, not causal importance beyond this query.

layout_constraints.rule: Each scene reads from source/input on the left toward output/payoff on the right or center.
Q, K, and V cards must appear as role projections from the same token origins.
Rows represent queries and columns represent keys; do not rotate or relabel this relationship.
Hold camera still while labels and formula fragments are introduced.

beats.teaching_goal: Introduce attention as a context question before formulas.
beats.viewer_question: Which other tokens matter to the active token?

beats.teaching_goal: Show Q, K, and V as roles from the same token representations.
beats.viewer_question: What do Q, K, and V each do?

beats.teaching_goal: Show query-key comparison and scaling as score-field transformations.
beats.viewer_question: How do queries and keys become relevance scores?

beats.teaching_goal: Show softmax as normalization into proportions.
Show weighted values merging into a context vector.

object_states.state: sentence strip visible; one token active; neighbor relevance arcs absent initially
Q, K, and V cards aligned by source token
raw score heatmap populated by comparison traces
normalized bars visible with sum-to-one cue
weighted streams converge into context vector

transitions.technical_event: The active token asks which other tokens matter.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: Token identity persists while arcs are added.

transitions.technical_event: Tokens become Q, K, and V role cards.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: Each role card should visibly originate from its token.

transitions.timing_note: Stagger by role: Q then K then V.

transitions.technical_event: Queries compare against keys to populate scores.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: Comparison traces terminate in heatmap cells.

transitions.timing_note: Trace a few cells explicitly, then reveal the remaining grid.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: The matrix remains the same semantic object while values are scaled.

transitions.timing_note: Cool heatmap intensity while the scale term appears.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: One score row becomes one distribution over source tokens.

transitions.timing_note: Transform raw bars into normalized bars, then reveal sum-to-one.

transitions.technical_event: Attention weights scale value vectors and merge them.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: Bar magnitudes correspond to stream thickness.

transitions.timing_note: Route streams with proportional thickness and merge into context vector.

motion_primitives.misread_risk: Viewer may think attention starts after choosing one fixed token only.
A single arc would suggest hard selection.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Represent role projections from each token.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Viewer may think Q, K, and V are different input tokens.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Keep same-token role cards visually grouped.
Map query-key comparisons to heatmap cells.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Comparison lines must not imply causal flow from keys to queries.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Expose the full score field after examples.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Full matrix should preserve query-row and key-column semantics.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Represent scaling by sqrt(d_k) as reduced score sharpness.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Scaling may look cosmetic unless paired with formula highlight.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Connect visual cooling to the sqrt(d_k) formula term.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Do not show scale as changing matrix shape.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Show softmax producing weights that sum to one.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Viewer may think raw score differences are preserved linearly.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Send value contributions toward output with weight-coded thickness.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Streams are weighted contributions, not physical particles.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Merge weighted values into the context vector.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Do not collapse into one selected value.

complexity_budget.notes: Introduce one conceptual operation per beat; formula fragments should appear only when their visual operation is active.

shots.hold_reason: Viewer must parse sentence strip and active token.
Hold once heatmap labels are readable.
Softmax and sum-to-one cue require stable layout.
Use slight push only after stream directions are clear.
shots.depth_usage: 2.5D stream separation only; no scientific depth claim.

camera_intents.intent: Do not move camera while introducing readable math or labels.
Use slight push-in only to move from mechanism overview to payoff.

readability_constraints: Formula labels must be rendered by deterministic code.
Heatmap row and column semantics must remain visible.
2.5D depth in value streams is only a separation device.

exact_required.reason: The formula is the source anchor.
Q/K/V labels and colors define the symbol ledger.
Toy softmax weights should sum to one exactly.

metaphor_allowed.allowed_scope: Stream visuals may make aggregation easier to see.
metaphor_allowed.boundary: Streams are weighted contributions, not physical flow.

metaphor_allowed.allowed_scope: Text-free spotlight or convergence backgrounds may support focus.
metaphor_allowed.boundary: No readable formula, labels, token text, or fake symbols.

ai_video_forbidden: Claims about training or multi-head attention

simplifications.statement: Scores and weights use toy values.
simplifications.impact: The animation teaches relationships, not a real model trace.
simplifications.disclosure_or_guardrail: Avoid wording that implies values came from a trained model.

simplifications.statement: The video focuses on one query position.
simplifications.impact: The full attention matrix is introduced through a representative row.
simplifications.disclosure_or_guardrail: Narration should say this is one token's attention question.

responsibilities: Render token cards, role splits, heatmap, softmax bars, and value streams.
Preserve deterministic object identity across transformations.
candidate_renderers.reason: The sample depends on readable formulas, labels, matrices, and deterministic transformations.
risks: Dense text can become unreadable if camera moves during labels.

responsibilities: Assemble rendered layers, subtitles, and audio timing.
candidate_renderers.reason: Timeline composition is separate from visual reasoning.
risks: Must not alter math-layer timing enough to desync narration.

responsibilities: Mux, transcode, and export final files.
risks: Alpha and frame-rate handling must be checked.

responsibilities: Generate optional text-free cinematic base layers or transitions.
candidate_renderers.reason: Can improve focus and atmosphere without owning exact math.
risks: May generate fake text or symbols if prompts are not constrained.

responsibilities: Prototype a 2.5D value-flow scene if needed.
candidate_renderers.reason: Useful if the attention sample later becomes interactive.

responsibilities: Polished 3D value-flow or abstract token space only if a 3D demo is required.
candidate_renderers.reason: Current sample does not require true 3D geometry.
risks: May introduce decorative 3D that implies nonexistent geometry.

layer_exactness_overrides.reason: The AI-generated support layer is a text-free cinematic metaphor even when it references exact-overlay object IDs for continuity.

fallbacks.tradeoff: Use flat deterministic backgrounds and transitions; scientific content remains intact.
Use schematic 2.5D value streams instead of polished 3D.

storyboard_beat_refs.storyboard_note: Start with the context question before introducing Q/K/V.
Emphasize that Q/K/V are roles from the same token representations.
Use heatmap as the bridge from comparison to scores, then scale.
Make softmax visible as normalization rather than magic.
End with the context vector as payoff.

enriched_scene_spec_hints.notes: AI support must be text-free; Manim owns token tiles and arcs.
No AI video layer needed because labels are exact.
Keep heatmap and scale term deterministic; AI support only as abstract transition.
Use deterministic toy weights that sum to one.
Value streams are metaphorical flow but thickness ordering should match weights.

downstream_tasks: Update storyboard scenes to reference dynamic beat IDs.
Move rendered outputs from site/public/videos to examples/attention/outputs.
Use exactness policy when generating provider-neutral AI video jobs.

source_fidelity_checks: Every Q/K/V visual object traces to the symbol ledger.
Scaling is connected to sqrt(d_k), not treated as decorative cooling.
Softmax is shown as normalization into weights.
Value aggregation uses multiple value contributions.
visual_readability_checks: Q/K/V labels are readable and stable.
Formula fragments are not rotated or placed in moving backgrounds.
misconception_checks: Attention is not presented as hard selection.
Q/K/V are not presented as different input tokens.
AI-generated layers do not contain fake formulas or labels.
Toy scores are not implied to be real model activations.
renderer_acceptance_checks: Manim renders exact math and labels.
Any AI video output is text-free and non-symbolic.
Assembly preserves scene order and beat timing.
Fallback without AI video still explains the mechanism.

open_questions.question: Should a future version compute toy Q/K/V vectors to generate real softmax values for the demo?

known_risks.risk: The sample could bias future schemas toward attention-specific heatmaps.
known_risks.mitigation: Keep Q/K/V as instances of generic entities/operators/transitions and test the schema on a second non-attention mechanism.

known_risks.risk: 2.5D value streams could imply physical depth that does not exist in the formula.
known_risks.mitigation: State depth is only for separation and keep formula/weights in exact 2D layers.

known_risks.risk: AI video support may generate fake labels or equations.
known_risks.mitigation: Use text-free keyframes, strict negative prompts, and deterministic Manim overlays.
